title: William M. Tweed
content: William Magear Tweed (April 3, 1823 – April 12, 1878), often erroneously referred to as "William "Marcy" Tweed" (see below), and widely known as "Boss" Tweed, was an American politician most notable for being the "boss" of Tammany Hall, the Democratic Party political machine that played a major role in the politics of 19th-century New York City and State.
